title: Resource Graphs, Cache Evidence, Budgets, and Performance Non-Claims · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent community information owner supplies a different origin, HTML structure, rate contract, accessibility need, resource ceiling, and injected failure and transfers resource graphs, cache evidence, budgets, and performance non-claims into a changed static resource and delivery evidence dossier. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of static resource and delivery evidence dossier with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, ex…
objectives: Inventory document, style, script, image, font, media, module, preload, and third-party references with source and scope.
Separate wire, decoded, retained, intrinsic-media, rendered, and duplicate-transfer sizes with missing evidence visible.
Report cache fields, validators, variation, redirects, compression, and cold or warm assumptions without simulating browser cache blindly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Resource Reference Graph
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 6663 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource graph, size dimensions, cache evidence, budgets, and performance non-claims; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inventory document, style, script, image, font, media, module, preload, and third-party references with source and scope.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Resource Reference Graph.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inventory document, style, script, image, font, media, module, preload, and third-party references with source and scope. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Resource Reference Graph, keep Resource Reference Graph connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inventory document, style, script, image, font, media, module, preload, and third-party references with source and scope.

For commit to the mission model, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.
